title: hedysarum boreale subsp. mackenziei
intro: Hedysarum boreale subsp. mackenziei, also known as Mackenzie's sweetvetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Hedysarum boreale subsp. mackenziei include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the dried roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent.
